items.title: 200 homes razed in pre-dawn Sandakan fire
items.summary: SANDAKAN: A pre-dawn fire destroyed about 200 homes in Kampung Bahagia here today. The Sabah Fire and Rescue Department received a distress call at 1.32am and arrived at 1.48am after travelling 15km from the station. In a statement, the department said narrow access routes, low tide, strong winds and closely built homes caused the fire to spread rapidly and hampered firefighting efforts.

items.title: Hizbollah rejects one-sided truce, vows response to Israel
items.summary: BEIRUT: Hizbollah leader Naim Qassem said on Saturday that the ongoing 10-day truce with Israel cannot be one-sided, vowing that his fighters would respond to Israeli attacks on Lebanon. Qassem called it "an insult to our country and our homeland, Lebanon, that America dictates its text and speaks on behalf of the Lebanese government." Qassem expressed "gratitude" to Iran "for their support and assistance", saying the country kept the Strait of Hormuz closed until a truce was reached in Lebanon.

items.title: Help University blast victim hangs on to UK dream
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A month after regaining consciousness, the victim of an air-conditioning compressor explosion at Help University still has no recollection of the incident. Reeya Kaur Thandal, 19, was supposed to fly to the UK in September, but the plan had to be postponed. "I read messages from a WhatsApp group where someone said it was something like an explosion in my class.

items.title: Task force to pursue asset forfeiture, tax recovery in e-waste blitz
items.summary: Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) Chief Commissioner Tan Sri Azam Baki said this would be among the issues raised when the MATF convenes next week to further coordinate enforcement efforts under Op Hazard. Azam said closer coordination and intelligence sharing among enforcement bodies would be critical in sustaining momentum against organised syndicates involved in the e-waste trade. Azam said the task force's move to rope in the police's Internal Security and Public Order Department to enhance intelligence sharing and enforcement beyond port areas was a positive development.

items.title: Authorities plug loophole to stop repatriated e-waste containers from returning to Malaysia
items.summary: PORT KLANG: Authorities have moved to plug a critical loophole in the country's fight against illegal electronic waste (e-waste) by leveraging a nationwide "selective list" system to ensure repatriated containers do not slip back into Malaysian ports. Port Klang Malaysian Border Control and Protection Agency (MCBA) commander Datuk Nik Ezanee Mohd Faisal said the mechanism, maintained by the Customs Department, was being actively used by the Port Klang Joint Task Force on E-waste (JTF) to track high-risk containers across all ports and entry points. Nik Ezanee said the use of the selective list strengthened enforcement against so-called "U-turn" containers — shipments rejected and later rerouted back into Malaysia using different ports or falsified documentation.

items.title: Prescribing growth: Private hospitals drive Malaysia’s RM7b medical tourism ambition
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 19 — Malaysia’s private healthcare sector has evolved into a global medical tourism powerhouse, attracting 1.84 million international patients and generating RM3.34 billion in revenue in 2025 alone. According to Datuk Dr Kuljit Singh, President of the Association of Private Hospitals Malaysia (APHM), approximately 80 of the nation’s 212 private hospitals are now actively engaged in medical tourism, offering services specifically tailored to an international clientele. Affordability only half the story While quality is a prerequisite, affordability remains Malaysia’s “X-factor.” “Malaysia’s position as a leading medical tourism destination is driven by the strong cost-competitiveness of our private healthcare sector,” Dr Kuljit noted.

items.title: In rural Malaysia, a first for an Orang Asli village: Constant power without the grid
items.summary: KLUANG, April 19 — Berasau Orang Asli Village here has made history by becoming the first remote Orang Asli settlement in the country to receive 24‑hour electricity through an integrated solar system, without relying on the public grid. The project, under the Ministry of Energy Transition and Water Transformation (Petra) through its Madani adopted village initiative, also includes a solar‑roofed community hall and local activity hub, while providing benefits to more than 20 households in the village, located far from major roads. Berasau Orang Asli community chief Sari Achu, 51, said residents can now enjoy basic amenities they have long waited for.

items.title: Iran shuts Hormuz again as Trump warns of 'blackmail'
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran's military declared the Strait of Hormuz closed again on Saturday, prompting ships to abandon attempts to transit and President Donald Trump to warn Teheran against trying to "blackmail" the United States. On Friday, Teheran had declared the strait, which usually carries a fifth of the world's oil and liquefied natural gas, open after a ceasefire was agreed in Israel's war with Iran's ally Hizbollah in Lebanon. "We'll be talking about Iran later.

items.title: Israeli army demolishes homes in south Lebanon town
items.summary: BEIRUT: Israeli forces on Saturday carried out demolitions in the southern Lebanese town of Bint Jbeil, the scene of intense fighting with Hizbollah prior to the recently agreed 10-day truce, Lebanese state media reported. "The Israeli enemy is repeating its house detonating operations in the town of Bint Jbeil," Lebanon's state-run National News Agency (NNA) said, also reporting demolitions in other border towns where Israeli troops are present. Bint Jbeil, located around five kilometres north of the Israeli border, had been the scene of heavy fighting between Israeli forces and Hizbollah for days before the truce went into force at midnight on Thursday.

items.title: Iran death toll rises to 3,468 in war with US, Israel
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran's state-run Foundation of Martyrs and Veterans Affairs said on Saturday that the war with the United States and Israel had killed more than 3,400 people in the Islamic republic. A previous toll from the head of the Iranian Legal Medicine Organisation issued on April 12 said 3,375 people in Iran had been killed in the war. The US-based Human Rights Activists News Agency (HRANA) said on April 7 that at least 3,636 people had been killed, including 1,701 civilians – among them at least 254 children – as well as 1,221 military personnel and 714 people whose status had not been classified.

items.title: Israel sets Gaza-style 'Yellow Line' in southern Lebanon
items.summary: JERUSALEM: Israel's military said Saturday it had established a "Yellow Line" in southern Lebanon, similar to the one separating its forces from Hamas-held areas in Gaza, adding it had hit a "fighter cell" operating along the demarcation. In its first mention of such a demarcation in Lebanon, the army said Saturday that its troops in the south "identified fighters who violated the ceasefire understandings and approached the forces from north of the Yellow Line in a manner that posed an immediate threat." Israel and Lebanon agreed to a 10-day ceasefire on Thursday to allow for negotiations to end six weeks of war between Israel and the Iran-backed group Hizbollah.

items.title: Iran Guards warn ships nearing Hormuz will be targeted
items.summary: TEHERAN: The navy of Iran's Revolutionary Guards warned on Saturday that any ship approaching the Strait of Hormuz would be treated as a target. Any attempt to approach the Strait of Hormuz will be considered cooperation with the enemy, and the offending vessel will be targeted," said the statement published on the Guards' official Sepah News website. --AFP © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: #NSTviral: Stranded rider vows to repay mechanic's kindness
items.summary: The rider, known only as Dicky, said he was deeply moved by the public response after his story spread on TikTok, which highlighted the mechanic's decision to assist him without taking payment. He was stranded and had nowhere else to turn," the mechanic said. The rider, however, said he could only afford RM80.

items.title: Police kill shooter in Kyiv, president says five people dead
items.summary: KYIV: Ukrainian police killed a man who opened fire in a district of Kyiv and barricaded himself into a supermarket on Saturday in an incident that killed at least five people. Mayor Vitali Klitschko, also writing on Telegram, said a woman among the 10 injured had died in hospital. "The shooter in Kyiv was liquidated during the arrest," Klymenko said on Telegram.

items.title: Iran says reviewing 'new proposals' from US
items.summary: TEHERAN: Iran's top national security body said Saturday that the country was reviewing "new proposals" received from the US, even as it warned that its negotiators would cut no compromises with Washington. "In recent days, with the presence of the commander of the Pakistani army in Teheran as an intermediary and mediator in the negotiations, new proposals have been put forward by the Americans, which the Islamic Republic of Iran is currently reviewing and has not yet responded to," the Supreme National Security Council said in a statement carried by state media. The Supreme National Security Council said "Iran is determined to enforce supervision and control over traffic through the Strait of Hormuz until the definitive end of the war", insisting the US blockade would preclude even a "conditional and limited reopening."

items.title: Spirit of goodwill, media collaboration must be strengthened - Bernama chairman
items.summary: MELAKA: The spirit of goodwill and cooperation between the media and community organisations must continue to be strengthened in addressing current challenges, including the spread of misinformation and global uncertainties. "The media does not merely deliver news, but shapes perceptions, nurtures empathy and connects society," he said when speaking at the Media Appreciation Night organised by the Melaka Civil Defence Force Associate Officers Association (PPBAPM) here last night. He said media coverage of Melaka PPBAPM's various initiatives, including humanitarian missions, emergency response and community training, has helped raise public awareness and strengthen the spirit of togetherness among society.

items.title: Sultan of Pahang urges caution during water activities in high-risk areas
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Sultan of Pahang, Al-Sultan Abdullah Ri'ayatuddin Al-Mustafa Billah Shah, and the Tengku Ampuan of Pahang, Tunku Azizah Aminah Maimunah Iskandariah, have urged the public to remain vigilant when engaging in water activities, particularly in high-risk areas such as beaches, rivers and recreational water sites. Their Royal Highnesses also stressed the importance of prioritising safety, including wearing life jackets when at sea or in open waters, and avoiding unsupervised or unsafe activities. "Parents and guardians are also urged to closely monitor the movements and safety of their children, especially near water, to prevent any untoward incidents," they said in a post on the Pahang Sultanate's Facebook page tonight.

items.title: Two killed in separate crashes in Perak
items.summary: IPOH: Two men were killed in separate incidents at Km281.1 (southbound) of the North-South Expressway (PLUS) and in Kampung Sungai Ranggam, Teluk Intan, today. District police chief Assistant Commissioner Muhammad Najib Hamzah said in the first incident this morning, a 21-year-old motorcyclist was killed after colliding with a lorry. He said the motorcyclist sustained severe injuries and was pronounced dead at the scene.

items.title: Fadhlina extends condolences to the family of drowned teen
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Education Minister Fadhlina Sidek has conveyed her condolences to the family of Luqman Kamil Jamaluddin, 16, who was found drowned after being reported missing while searching for clams at Balok Beach, Pahang, yesterday. In a Facebook post today, Fadhlina said the Education Ministry's (MoE) immediate priority was to ensure that appropriate assistance is extended to the family. "My condolences go out to the entire family.

items.title: Sanchez emerges as progressive star against Trump chaos
items.summary: Spain's Pedro Sanchez hosted a summit of world progressives last week with greater global clout as turbulent relations with US President Donald Trump bolster his image as a left-wing hero. Sanchez broke with Nato allies last year by refusing to agree to Trump's demand that alliance members hike defence spending to five per cent of GDP. Sanchez has gained attention in international media, penning articles for the New York Times and Le Monde diplomatique.

items.title: RM28.8mil allocated to boost Malaysia's halal industry under 13MP
items.summary: He said the allocation this year had been channelled to the Entrepreneur Development and Cooperatives Ministry, the Department of Islamic Development Malaysia (Jakim) and the Halal Development Corporation (HDC). Among the approved initiatives are programmes focusing on halal innovation and technology development, facilitation of halal certification, and strengthening halal integrity, including the enhancement of Jakim's My e-Halal system. "With the funding and financing support provided, halal industry players are expected to build their business capacity and, in turn, contribute to the development of the national halal industry," he said.

items.title: The dark side of art that fuels money laundering
items.summary: The very nature of art makes it an attractive vehicle for money laundering. Global money laundering is estimated to range between US$600 billion and US$1.5 trillion annually, accounting for about two to five per cent of global GDP. The three stages of artwork-based money laundering unfold in distinct ways.

items.title: Some tankers cross Strait of Hormuz before shots fired
items.summary: OSLO: More than a dozen tankers, including three sanctioned vessels, passed through the Strait of Hormuz after a 50-day blockade was lifted on Friday, shipping data showed, before Iran reimposed restrictions on Saturday and fired at some vessels. US President Donald Trump said on Friday Iran had agreed to open the strait, while Iranian officials said they wanted the US to fully lift its blockade of Iranian tankers. IRAN RESUMES RESTRICTIONS The ships that passed through the strait on Friday and Saturday via Iranian waters south of Larak island were mainly older, non-Western-owned vessels and included four sanctioned ships, according to ship-tracking data.

items.title: NST Leader: How to put idle federal land to good use
items.summary: Idle federal land has long been an issue for Putrajaya. Putrajaya's move to re-examine undeveloped federal land is a step in the right direction. Firstly, a centralised federal land registry with real-time data must be established.

items.title: Trump says Iran cannot 'blackmail us' with Strait of Hormuz
items.summary: WASHINGTON: US President Donald Trump on Saturday warned Iran not to "blackmail" Washington with its flip-flopping on the fate of the Strait of Hormuz, after Teheran declared the strategic waterway once again closed. They wanted to close up the strait again – you know, as they've been doing for years – and they can't blackmail us," Trump said at a White House event. Trump said there would be "some information" about Iran later in the day, adding: "We're taking a tough stand."

items.title: First group of Malaysian haj pilgrims safely arrive in Madinah
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The first group of Malaysian haj pilgrims for the 1447H/2026 season safely arrived at Prince Mohammad Abdulaziz International Airport in Madinah at 8.21am local time (1.21pm Malaysian time) today. All 284 pilgrims departed on Malaysia Airlines flight MH8050 from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) at 4.09am (Malaysian time) and arrived at Hotel Emaar Royal in Madinah at 9.45am (local time). TH said two more flights carrying Malaysian pilgrims were expected to arrive in Madinah today, with 284 pilgrims at 2.10pm and another 357 at 9.10pm local time.

items.title: Govt assures B20 biodiesel safe for vehicles, no compatibility issues
items.summary: PORT KLANG: The government has given assurances that there are no compatibility issues arising from the use of biodiesel, particularly at B20 levels, following the implementation of engine specifications supporting the fuel since 2020. Economy Minister Akmal Nasrullah Mohd Nasir said that with the standards made mandatory, the use of biodiesel should not cause damage to vehicles. He said this at a press conference after visiting the KL-Kepong Oleomas Sdn Bhd oleochemical plant together with Plantation and Commodities Minister Datuk Seri Dr Noraini Ahmad today.

items.title: Today's news in pictures – Saturday, April 18, 2026
items.summary: MISSED what happened today? Here's a glimpse at what happened throughout the country in photographs. [[nid:1420792]] [[nid:1420791]] [[nid:1420790]] [[nid:1420789]] [[nid:1420788]] [[nid:1420787]] [[nid:1420786]] [[nid:1420785]] [[nid:1420784]] © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Police investigate individual suspected of making defamatory statements against MAIS
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The police are investigating an individual who is believed to have made defamatory statements against the Selangor Islamic Religious Council (MAIS) and the Selangor Islamic Religious Department (JAIS) through social media posts. Selangor police chief Datuk Shazeli Kahar said this was following a report received by his department on April 16, which alleged that the content was created to tarnish the image of MAIS and JAIS. "The complainant alleged that the content of the video is defamatory as it makes accusations of corruption and the use of royal immunity, which appears to be deliberately made to tarnish the image of MAIS and JAIS," he said in a statement today.

items.title: UN peacekeeper killed, three wounded in south Lebanon shooting, Unifil says
items.summary: CAIRO/PARIS/GENEVA: A United Nations peacekeeper was killed and three others wounded after a Unifil patrol came under small-arms fire while clearing explosive ordnance along a road in the southern Lebanese village of Ghandouriyeh, the peacekeeping mission said on Saturday. Two of the wounded peacekeepers were seriously injured, it said. Unifil said initial assessments indicated the fire came from non-state actors, allegedly Hezbollah, and that an investigation had been launched into what it described as "a deliberate attack."

items.title: Two Indian ships 'forced back' from Hormuz Strait
items.summary: ISTANBUL: Two Indian ships were "forced back west out of the Strait of Hormuz" by the navy of Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC), according to tanker monitoring service data today, Anadolu Ajansi (AA) reported. The report is based on two Channel 16 (VHF 156.8 MHz) recordings, the international maritime distress and calling channel, TankerTrackers said on X. On Friday, Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi declared the strait "completely open" for all commercial vessels.

items.title: Two teenage girls among 12 held in Wakaf Bharu migrant smuggling raid
items.summary: TUMPAT: Two teenage girls, including one believed to have acted as a "tonto" (lookout), were detained in a migrant smuggling raid in Wakaf Bharu early today. Also arrested were nine undocumented migrants and a 24-year-old Malaysian man suspected of being linked to a human trafficking syndicate. General Operations Force (GOF) Southeast Brigade commander Senior Assistant Commissioner Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the group was detained during an operation at premises in Bandar Baru Pasir Pekan.

items.title: PM pays tribute to Karpal Singh, Sikh community, recalls hospital visits during detention
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today paid tribute to the late Karpal Singh and the Sikh community, highlighting their enduring role in Malaysia’s fight for justice and unity. Anwar said Malaysia’s strength lies in its diversity, with cultures and traditions forming “the very foundation” of national unity. Reflecting on his personal journey, Anwar said Sikh families had been part of his life, including during his years of political struggle, imprisonment and court battles.

items.title: Health minister says govt will ensure adequate supply of raw materials for medical devices
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 18 — The government is focused on ensuring adequate supplies of raw materials for medical devices, Health Minister Datuk Seri Dr Dzulkefly Ahmad said. He added that the price aspects related to the strategic agreement with China to purchase resin and naphtha were still at the negotiation stage ased on the concept of willing buyer, willing seller. "Ensure the supply, we secure first all related matters… the price is being negotiated, willing buyer, willing seller, there’s no problems.

items.title: Deputy youth minister: Dialogue between Higher Education Ministry and university student league to happen by May
items.summary: MELAKA, April 18 — A dialogue session between the Higher Education Ministry (MOHE) and Liga Mahasiswa Malaysia (LMM) is expected to take place by May at the latest, its deputy minister, Adam Adli Abdul Halim said. The organisation of the dialogue is at its final stages, including preparing a platform in the form of a roundtable discussion of periodic open session, to discuss issues relating to tertiary students. He said the dialogue was merely receiving and reading memoranda without further action, but will feature comprehensive discussions between various parties including university administrators, academics and students, adding that he wanted all voices to be really heard.

items.title: PKR denies allegations of going alone in Johor state election, committed to cooperating with partners
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 18 — PKR today strongly denied a report by a media portal claiming that the party would contest solo in the upcoming Johor state election. Johor PKR chairman Datuk Seri Dr Zaliha Mustafa described the claim as inaccurate, speculative and did not reflect the actual position of discussions at party or Pakatan Harapan (PH) level. She said any considerations regarding the strategy for facing the Johor state election would be determined through a structured consultation process within the party leadership and also discussed at the state and national PH levels.

items.title: Kelantan development allocation increased to RM8b, says PM
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 18 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today announced an increase in the federal government’s development allocation for Kelantan from RM3.19 billion to RM8 billion. Anwar, who is also Finance Minister, said the federal government allocated RM3.19 billion for the implementation of development projects in Kelantan last year, nearly double the RM1.55 billion allocated in 2022. Touching on national governance, the Prime Minister stressed that the MADANI Government remains firm in not allowing any form of abuse of power or misappropriation of national assets.

items.title: Watch out for storms in KL, 10 other states until 8pm, says MetMalaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Thunderstorms, heavy rain and strong winds are expected to hit the capital and 10 other states until 8pm today. The Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), in a statement today, said the affected states are Perlis; Kedah (Langkawi, Kubang Pasu, Kota Setar, Sik, Baling, Kulim and Bandar Baharu); Penang; Perak; Kelantan (Jeli and Kuala Krai); and Terengganu (Hulu Terengganu). A similar weather warning has also been issued for Pahang, involving Jerantut, Maran, Bera and Rompin; Selangor (Sabak Bernam, Kuala Selangor, Hulu Selangor, Klang, Gombak, Petaling and Kuala Langat); and Melaka.

items.title: Khairy contesting in Kedah? No problem, says MB Sanusi
items.summary: ALOR SETAR, April 18 — Kedah is open to any political figure wishing to contest in the state's elections, including former Umno Youth Chief Khairy Jamaluddin, said Perikatan Nasional (PN) Kedah chairman and Kedah Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Muhammad Sanusi Md Nor. "Anyone can come and contest in Kedah. Kedah is open to anyone," he said.

items.title: Stay cool: Seven areas in Peninsular Malaysia under level 1 hot weather alert
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Seven areas in Peninsular Malaysia were placed under Level 1 hot weather alert, or caution status, as of 4pm today, according to the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia). It said the affected areas includes the whole of Perlis, Setiu in Terengganu, and five districts in Kelantan, namely Machang, Kuala Krai, Pasir Mas, Jeli and Tanah Merah. The public may refer to MetMalaysia’s official website at www.met.gov.my/iklim/status-cuaca-panas/ for the latest daily hot weather status nationwide.

items.title: Perlis MB urged to explain last minute postponement of state assembly meeting
items.summary: KANGAR, April 18 — Several state assemblymen have urged Perlis Menteri Besar Abu Bakar Hamzah to give an immediate explanation over the postponement of the state legislative assembly session, which had been scheduled from April 21 to 23. Sanglang state assemblyman and former Perlis PAS senior leader Mohd Shukri Ramli said he and five other PAS reps received yesterday’s postponement notice without any reasonable explanation. “(The Menteri Besar) must explain as quickly as possible… he knows the reason he postponed this (state assembly) session,” he said at a press conference at the Mata Ayer constituency service centre here today, which was also attended by the other five PAS assemblymen.

items.title: In return to Umno, Khairy says no demands, ready to rebuild trust in public
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Umno returnee Khairy Jamaluddin today expressed his gratitude and readiness to work to restore public confidence in the party that expelled him three years ago. In a Facebook post this afternoon, he thanked Umno president Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi and the party supreme council for approving his readmission. “I am ready to work with all levels of leadership and grassroots members to restore public confidence in the party,” he said.

items.title: Digital Ministry: Backlog of 3,600 foreign knowledge worker applications cleared, processing to stabilise by Q2
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 18 — A backlog of over 3,600 Foreign Knowledge Worker (FKW) applications has been processed at Malaysian Digital Economic Corporation stage and have been forwarded to the next stage for further action as of April 15, the Digital Ministry said today. It added that the backlog was due to several factors including technological system constraints requiring manual processing, limited workforce resources, and a sharp increase in application volumes, and that it had also affected programmes such as De Rantau Nomad and the Malaysia Tech Entrepreneur Programme (MTEP). It has also begun procuring a new digital platform that will integrate the three programmes - FKW, De Rantau Nomad and MTEP - to prevent recurring issues,” the ministry said.

items.title: Asia’s busiest trade route, the Straits of Malacca, draws scrutiny as security risks rise
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Rising geopolitical tensions linked to the Strait of Hormuz are putting renewed attention on the Straits of Malacca, one of the world’s most critical maritime trade routes. The narrow waterway that passes Malaysia, Indonesia, and Singapore connects the Indian and Pacific Oceans and carries a significant share of global trade, including energy supplies bound for major Asian economies. The strait is also routinely monitored by international naval forces, including the US reflecting its strategic importance in global security planning.

items.title: What you need to know: Why Malaysia still imports oil and how the Hormuz disruption is driving up costs
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Malaysia produces crude oil, but it still relies heavily on imports to meet domestic fuel demand that is nearly double local output, while global supply disruptions are also pushing up costs across the oil supply chain. Malaysia continues to operate a “sell high, buy low” strategy, exporting higher-value crude while importing other grades of oil to ensure sufficient supply for domestic consumption. Why does Malaysia still import oil despite being a producer?

items.title: Caltex Malaysia announces temporary fuel supply disruption in five states due to port delay
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Caltex Malaysia has announced that several of its service stations across five states may experience temporary disruptions in the supply of RON95 petrol and diesel over the coming days, beginning yesterday. In an Instagram post, Caltex stated that the affected states are Perlis, Kedah, Penang, Perak, and Kelantan. Caltex also apologised for any inconvenience caused and thanked customers for their patience and understanding.

items.title: Malaysia link emerges in sexual abuse and conversion scandal at global IT giant TCS in India
items.summary: NASHIK (India), April 18 — Authorities in India have uncovered a Malaysia-based link in a scandal involving allegations of sexual harassment and forced religious conversion at global IT services giant Tata Consultancy Services (TCS). Digital evidence from WhatsApp chats and video calls linked “Imran” to the wider network are currently under investigation. Indian police said the investigation began after a covert operation in which female officers posed as cleaning staff to gather evidence inside the premises.

items.title: PM: 85pc of Malaysians paying subsidised diesel rates, including fishermen, school bus services
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 18 — Eighty-five per cent of Malaysians are unaffected by market diesel prices because they still enjoy the government’s subsidised rates, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. Eighty-five per cent of the people are not using diesel at the market price; they use subsidised prices. “Fishermen use subsidised diesel prices, not market diesel prices.

items.title: PM Anwar says Petronas negotiating with Russia for oil is an option, domestic needs priority over supplying other nations
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 18 — Petroliam Nasional Berhad (Petronas) can negotiate with Russia for oil supply as a proactive measure to ensure the country’s energy reserves remain sufficient for public consumption, said Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. He said this step can be taken as many European countries, which along with the United States previously imposed various sanctions on Moscow, are now again competing to secure oil supplies from Russia due to current economic needs. Anwar said geopolitical tensions involving Iran, the United States and Europe have a direct impact on the global transport sector as oil prices and fertiliser supplies are affected.

items.title: BN to refine seat formula but stick to existing framework, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today urged party machinery to begin groundwork immediately for upcoming elections, saying timing is secondary to preparedness on the ground. “Whether state or general elections are held sooner or later is not the issue. We must go out and reach voters beyond our base.” Speaking after attending the Malaysian Indian Muslim Congress (KIMMA) 46th annual general meeting at the World Trade Centre Kuala Lumpur, Zahid said early mobilisation is key as BN prepares for state polls and the next general election.

items.title: PM Anwar: Malaysia stands firm in speaking truth on global stage
items.summary: KOTA BHARU, April 18 — Malaysia remains firm in its independent and sovereign position to speak the truth at the international level, even if it is difficult for some countries and major world powers to accept, said Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim. The Prime Minister said this courageous stance is reflected in Malaysia’s position on the West Asian conflict, particularly the United States-backed Israeli attacks on Iran. "In my discussion with the President of Iran (Masoud Pezeshkian), he expressed appreciation to the federal government and the Malaysian Parliament for condemning the attacks by Israel and the United States, and for giving strong support in safeguarding the right to freedom in Iran.

items.title: Man jailed for using fake payslip to buy products on instalment
items.summary: KOTA KINABALU, April 18 — The Magistrate’s Court here sentenced a 37-year-old man, who used a forged payslip as genuine to purchase electrical items on instalment, to nine months in jail. Magistrate Wan Farrah Farriza Wan Ghazali meted out the sentence to Nikolaus Owin Yohanes Laba after he pleaded guilty to dishonestly using a payslip in his name purportedly issued by an auto accessories company, knowing it to be false, on July 3, 2023, at 12.54pm, at Kwang Woon Trading Sdn Bhd in Taman Indah Permai, Manggatal. Deputy Public Prosecutor Akaash Singh informed the court that Nikolaus had purchased a television and an air-conditioner on instalment from a shop, using the payslip as supporting documentation.

items.title: Man fined RM48,000 for possessing carcasses, live specimen of various protected species
items.summary: SANDAKAN, April 18 — A 38-year-old was fined RM48,000 after pleading guilty to a charge of possessing animals from protected species at the Sessions Court here on Thursday. Hendry Rayner Endim, a plantation officer, pleaded guilty after the charge was read before Sessions Court Judge Roslinda Razali. These animals are classified as protected species under the Wildlife Conservation Enactment 1997.

items.title: MIC still ‘core’ member of BN, says Zahid
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today asserted that MIC is still a component member of the coalition, amid speculations over its status. “They are still core members of BN,” he told reporters when asked about MIC’s status after officiating at the AGM of BN ally, Malaysian Indian Muslim Congress at the World Trade Centre here. At its assembly last November, MIC delegates voted to leave BN.

items.title: Sudden lane change suspected in fatal Miri crash involving sports coupe and lorry
items.summary: MIRI, April 18 — A sudden lane change is believed to have caused a fatal accident along Jalan Miri Bypass yesterday morning, said Miri District police chief ACP Mohd Farhan Lee Abdullah. He said the accident involved a two-door sports coupe driven by a 40-year-old local man and a lorry driven by a 34-year-old local man. The sports coupe, which was travelling from Permyjaya towards Miri Airport, is suspected to have suddenly changed lanes from the right to the left side of the road.

items.title: Fahmi: Govt to streamline anti-scam efforts with new laws, joint agency session soon
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — A special session to update all relevant stakeholders on current developments related to online scams will be held the soonest, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil had said today. “We have been working closely with the relevant stakeholders on any matters relating to scams and in fact our last meeting was held last week. “It isn’t just fraud, but all kinds of online scams be it investment scams, love scams and many various other types of scams that has been abusing official channels.

items.title: Zahid backs Kimma entry into BN, but says other components must agree ‘100pc’ first
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today said he is open to accepting the Malaysian Indian Muslim Congress (Kimma) as a full member. He said Kimma supporters need to prove themselves to the BN supreme council and make a formal application. He said the BN must present a cohesive front.

items.title: Kimma renews push for BN inclusion, raises worker shortage woes at AGM
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Malaysian Indian Muslim Congress (Kimma) president Datuk Seri Syed Ibrahim Kader today renewed calls for the party to be formally accepted as a component of Barisan Nasional (BN), questioning the delay despite decades of loyalty to the coalition. But the question remains — why are we still not a component party?” he said. This is our country,” he said.

items.title: Ready for orders on ministers’ salary cuts, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — Cabinet ministers are ready to accept any orders on salary cuts, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said. He said the matter was discussed, however it depends on Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s call. “I’m ready to accept any orders from the Prime Minister.

items.title: Tanker with one million barrels of crude oil from Iraq arrives in Malaysia
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 18 — The Petronas vessel Ocean Thunder has arrived in Malaysia carrying one million barrels of crude oil from Basrah, Iraq. The shipment forms part of a broader supply network managed by the national oil company to ensure stable fuel availability nationwide amid a disruption in the Strait of Hormuz amid West Asia tensions. “Malaysia's fuel supply is drawn from multiple sources across Petronas' wider supply network.
